data.title: NYC legal aid union settles with lawyers who claimed anti-Israel bias
data.description:
By David Thomas

A union representing New York-based legal aid lawyers said Thursday it will pay $315,000 to settle claims that it tried to retaliate against three of its members after they challenged the union's issuance of a resolution calling for a ceasefire in Gaza and "an end to Israeli apartheid."

As part of the settlement, the Association of Legal Aid Attorneys, UAW Local 2325, will issue a statement acknowledging that "some of the communications" around the discussion of the resolution in November and December 2023 "were hurtful to union members and inappropriate."

The union also agreed that it will provide training to its executive board members on the rights of labor union members, and the union agreed to refer any disciplinary charges it files against its members to its outside counsel for review until 2028, according to settlement papers filed Thursday in Manhattan federal court.

"This settlement is a landmark in the fight against anti-Semitism in this sector," Kenneth Marcus, the chairman of the nonprofit Louis D. Brandeis Center for Human Rights Under Law, which represented the plaintiffs, said in a statement.

Attorneys for the ALAA did not immediately respond to a request for comment.

The ALAA is an affiliate of the United Auto Workers union and represents lawyers who work for New York-area groups that represent indigent defendants including the Legal Aid Society. The ALAA on its website says its membership includes more than 3,000 lawyers and advocates.

At the time the lawsuit was filed in July 2024, the plaintiffs were lawyers who worked at the Legal Aid Society of Nassau County. Plaintiffs Ilana Kopmar, Diane Clarke and Isaac Altman called the resolution "blatantly anti-Semitic" and sought a court order stopping the union from disciplining or expelling them for mounting an earlier, unsuccessful lawsuit that sought to block it.

The union denied wrongdoing, and had argued that the plaintiffs "tried and failed to interfere with union democracy and chill union members' protected speech."

data.title: Front Month ICE Brent Crude Rose 0.48% to Settle at $63.01 — Data Talk
data.description:
Front Month ICE Brent Crude for Jan. delivery gained 30 cents per barrel, or 0.48% to $63.01 today

  • Up four of the past five sessions
  • Off 23.19% from its 52-week high of $82.03 hit Wednesday, Jan. 15, 2025
  • Up 4.62% from its 52-week low of $60.23 hit Monday, May 5, 2025
  • Down 13.16% from 52 weeks ago
  • Off 23.19% from its 2025 settlement high of $82.03 hit Wednesday, Jan. 15, 2025
  • Up 4.62% from its 2025 settlement low of $60.23 hit Monday, May 5, 2025
  • Off 56.87% from its record high of $146.08 hit Thursday, July 3, 2008
  • Month-to-date it is down 3.17%
  • Year-to-date it is down $11.63 or 15.58%

All prices are calculated based on the settlement price of the current front month contract.

Source: Dow Jones Market Data

data.title: PLATTS: 100--Data center power demand leading to supply shortages, high prices: PJM IMM
data.description: (Platts)--13Nov2025/317 pm EST/2017 GMT **Data centers should bring generation **Fast-track connection queue needed The PJM Interconnection should create a queue for data centers that would not be connected to the grid until there is adequate capacity, and there should be a fast-track queue process for data centers that bring their own generation, PJM's independent market monitor said in its latest state of the market report released Nov. 13. In August, PJM's management board initiated an accelerated stakeholder process to address the anticipated power demand growth from data centers, which could reach 30 GW by 2030. The grid operator aims to submit any necessary filings to federal regulators by December 2025. There are roughly 20 proposals being considered. Monitoring Analytics, PJM's IMM, has taken the position that data center load growth has become unsustainable, resulting in reliability concerns as well as elevated energy and capacity prices. "There are clear warning signs for the capacity market," the market watchdog said in the state-of-the-market report. "The capacity market was short of meeting its reliability objective in the most recent capacity auction for the 2026-2027 base residual auction." The grid operator was also short of meeting its installed reserve margin target as of June 1, 2025, on an installed capacity and an unforced capacity basis, the IMM said. And while the volume that PJM is short of capacity is "relatively small, around 200 MW in both the BRA and the actual available capacity," the report said. "The price impacts have been very large and will be even larger in the near term even if the issues are addressed in a timely manner," according to the report. "Data center load growth is the primary reason for recent and expected capacity market conditions, including total forecast load growth, the tight supply and demand balance, and high prices," the IMM said. If it were not for data center power demand growth — both actual and forecast — the capacity market would not have experienced the same tight supply demand conditions, the same high prices observed in the 2025-2026 BRA and the 2026-2027 BRA, and the currently expected tight supply conditions and high prices for subsequent capacity auctions, the market monitor said. CAPACITY REVENUE, PRICE INCREASES Including existing and forecast data center load growth increased capacity market revenues for the 2025-2026 BRA and the 2026-2027 BRA by a total of $16,603,301,829, which will continue to grow until the issues associated with the additions of large data center loads are addressed, according to the report. "The impact will increase significantly in the 2028-2029 BRA currently scheduled for June 2026, when the maximum and minimum prices defined by the Agreement are no longer effective," the IMM said. Specifically, customers are being impacted by higher transmission costs, higher energy market prices and higher capacity market prices. "It is clear that continuing to simply accept the interconnection of large data center loads that cannot be served reliably because there is not adequate dispatchable capacity, is not a reasonable path forward and is not a market solution and is not a solution of any kind," the market watchdog said. Continuing to operate the power market in that fashion would result in continued supply shortfalls, increased reliability issues, and continued maximum power prices. The current supply of capacity in PJM is not adequate to meet the demand from large data center loads, and it will not be adequate in the foreseeable future, the market monitor said. The market solution is to establish a queue for adding large new data center loads which would not be interconnected until there is adequate capacity to serve them, and the market solution would create an expedited fast-track load and generation interconnection process for large data centers that bring their own new generation "with locational and temporal characteristics reasonably matched to their load profile," the IMM said. The energy market price impact of adding data center load without new generation would be in the billions of dollars annually, and the additional load without new generation would impact energy prices directly by requiring generation from more expensive resources on the supply curve and by increasing the probability of shortage pricing, the report said. PJM will hold a final accelerated stakeholder process meeting on Nov. 19. This meeting will be immediately followed by a special Members Committee meeting, during which an advisory vote on the proposals will be conducted for the PJM Board of Managers. After the Nov. 19 Members Committee meeting, the board will decide whether to move forward with any of the proposed solutions. If so, PJM will file the plan with the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission under Section 205 of the Federal Power Act. — Jared Anderson, jared.anderson@spglobal.com-- Edited by Marieke Alsguth, newsdesk@spglobal.com--Platts Electricity Alert--

data.title: Siemens Energy Raises FY28 Target View to Profit Margin Before Special Items 14%-16%
Gold Reverses Early Momentum
data.description:
Gold prices dropped toward $4,160 per ounce on Thursday after earlier touching three-week highs, amid a broad market sell-off that followed the US government reopening.

The initial rally was driven by expectations that delayed official data would reveal labor market weakness and push the Federal Reserve toward rate cuts, but those hopes ebbed as Treasury yields climbed, raising the opportunity cost of holding a non-yielding asset.

Recent comments from Fed officials reinforced that a December easing is not assured, and traders now assign roughly a 50% chance of a 25 basis point reduction next month.

Gold has still gained nearly 5% this week, yet today’s move looks like profit taking and a recalibration of policy expectations.

data.title: The Estate Tax Is Dead, This Expert Says. What Comes Next. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Emily Russell

Since Congress created the income and estate taxes, inequality in the U.S. has grown dramatically. The top 0.1% now hold roughly 15% of the country's wealth — a near doubling of wealth concentration since 1990.

That this growing wealth divide is an issue is a widely shared opinion on both the left and the right. What the root of the problem is, and how best to correct it, famously is not.

Ray Madoff, a tax law professor at Boston College of Law has been studying this problem for decades. In her new book, The Second Estate, she blames the wealth gap largely on a failing estate tax.

Congress created the estate tax in 1916, in part, to correct the fact that the income tax system leaves a lot of potential revenue from wealthy families on the table. Income taxes only reach those with taxable incomes. The ultrawealthy, Madoff points out in her book, don't make most of their money through wages or salaries.

"But the estate tax is no longer up to the task because Congress hasn't kept it up," she tells Barron's in a recent phone interview. "It is this sort of shell — a completely decomposing, ineffective thing."

The last time Congress acted to fix what it saw as loopholes in the tax was 1990, Madoff says. Policy changes since then have generally weakened the provision. Legislators from both parties have increased exemption amounts in recent decades.

"What in effect has happened behind the scenes is that the richest Americans who were the intended object of taxation when the system was put in place have been able to remove themselves from the system," she says.

We shouldn't try to fix this by tinkering further with the estate tax, but by killing it altogether, she argues.

The following transcript of Barron's conversation with Madoff has been edited.

Barron's : The estate tax was largely successful in moderating wealth inequality for much of the 20th century. But then public opinion shifted against the tax in recent decades. How did that happen?

Ray Madoff: It didn't come out of thin air. A group of 18 wealthy families, including the Mars family, the Koch family, the Walton family, initiated a campaign in the 1990s to get rid of the estate tax. But that wasn't going to be a sell to the American public. Who would listen to wealthy heirs and heiresses?

So they hired Frank Luntz, one of the country's great public relations experts. (He is also responsible for popularizing the term "climate change" to replace "global warming").

Luntz said that people have no feelings about repealing the estate tax — it sounds like something that only applies to the rich. The thing to do is to use emotionally charged words, and there are a few more emotionally charged words than "death." So he invented the term "death tax." Lots of people care about repealing the death tax because death is universal, and it seems like a particularly cruel time to impose a tax.

How did that message get transposed onto the middle class?

Those pushing for the repeal found they could use small-business owners as a cover, as a front for the wealthy. They recruited the middle class to argue the death tax is an immoral double tax that hurts families, farms, and businesses. One of the great spokespeople was Chester Thigpen, who was the grandchild of slaves and owned a Christmas tree farm. He testified in Congress many times saying he was afraid that, with the death tax, his family was going to have to sell the Christmas tree farm. Could there be a more compelling story? But, of course, when Thigpen died, he wasn't even subject to the tax because there is a large exemption amount that covers most families, farms, and businesses.

That campaign was so successful that President George W. Bush significantly reduced the estate tax, and President Barack Obama later increased the exemption amount. What effect has that had?

Well, if that was all that happened, that wouldn't make such a difference because these exemption amounts [currently just under $14 million] are still pretty small relative to the wealth of the richest Americans. But a more significant thing has occurred: Since 1990, Congress hasn't enacted a single provision to close loopholes in the estate tax. That is why we no longer see the richest Americans even advocating for repeal of it any more. It is better for the wealthy with it being in place, because it provides cover — making it seem like they are subject to some big tax without actually imposing any burden.

You write that the narrative around taxing the rich is distorted, in part, because statistics are used in misleading ways. What statistics do you wish people knew?

In 2024, the government took in $5 trillion through taxes. It spent roughly $7 trillion. But at the end of 2024, the amount of wealth owned by the country's richest 1% was $50 trillion. So government revenue was tiny compared to the wealth of the top 1%. This definitely matters: Taxing the wealthy could make a difference in the government deficit.

There is little momentum to do that in Washington right now. The estate tax exemption doubled permanently, to $15 million, in the One Big, Beautiful Bill. Does that discourage you?

I am honestly encouraged by what is going on politically because I think we are reaching a tipping point. Our rules are so unfair in favor of the wealthy that I think the public is going to recognize it. I don't think it will ultimately be tolerated. We have seen pendulum swings like this before in other policy areas — and I just have to believe that it will swing in this area as well.

Toward Mamdani?

Zohran Mamdani is a great example of this. I differ from him over his proposed solutions, but I completely agree with his description of the problem.

But I don't think it is just Mamdani; it is also Trump. They both recognize that large portions of the American public have been left behind. There is something very real going on when everybody across the political spectrum is seeing the same problem. From my perspective, taxes play a critical role in both creating and addressing this problem. The issue is I don't think that the current proposals receiving the most oxygen are ones that will address the problem.

What would you propose?

We must recognize that the estate tax is dead and it is not coming back. So we should outright repeal it and then reform the income tax to bring back in investments and inheritances. Inheritances should be taxed as income, the same as found money or lottery winnings. You could have exemptions — everybody can inherit one million dollars tax-free, for instance.

Investments are trickier. Some propose taxing gains on investments even if the property hasn't been sold. But that type of tax would be difficult to administer and would have serious unintended consequences: If you're a rich person, the first thing you're going to do is move your assets out of the easy to value public stock market to private interests. But that doesn't mean that we say that these gains are never subject to tax. Gains should be taxed when the property is transferred, either during life or death. This was a proposal put forth by both President Richard Nixon and Obama.

Those steps would be important first steps that would do a lot to bring the wealthy back into our income tax system.

Write to editors@barrons.com.

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Notice of extraordinary general meeting in LIDDS AB (publ)
data.description:
The shareholders of LIDDS AB (publ), reg. no. 556580-2856, (the “Company” or “Lidds”), are hereby invited to an extraordinary general meeting on Thursday, 18 December 2025 at 10:00 CET at Excore's premises at Kungsportsavenyen 22, 411 36 Gothenburg.

Right to participate and notice to attend the meeting

Shareholders who are recorded in the share register kept by Euroclear Sweden AB (The Swedish Central Securities Depository Euroclear Sweden AB) as of 10 December 2025 and who have notified the Company of their intention to attend so that the notification is received by the Company no later than 12 December 2025 are entitled to participate in the meeting. The notification shall be sent to LIDDS AB (publ), Kungsportsavenyen 22, 411 36 Gothenburg. The notification may also be submitted electronically and shall then be sent to: info@liddspharma.com.

The notification shall state the full name, personal identification number or company registration number, shareholding, address, telephone number (daytime) and, where applicable, the number of assistants (maximum two). The notification should, where applicable, be accompanied by a power of attorney, certificate of registration, and other authorisation documents.

Nominee-registered shares

To be entitled to participate in the extraordinary general meeting, shareholders who have had their shares registered with a nominee must temporarily re-register the shares in their own name. Shareholders who wish to re-register their voting rights must notify its trustee well in advance before 12 December 2025. The re-registration must be effected with Euroclear Sweden AB on 12 December 2025.

Proxy etc.

If shareholders are to be represented by a proxy, the proxy must bring a written and dated power of attorney signed by the shareholder to the meeting. The power of attorney must not be older than one year, unless a longer validity period (but no more than five years) is specified in the power of attorney. If the power of attorney is issued by a legal entity, the proxy must also bring a current registration certificate or equivalent authorisation document for the legal entity. To facilitate entry at the meeting, a copy of the power of attorney and other authorisation documents should be attached to the notification of attendance at the general meeting. Power of attorney forms are available on the Company's website www.liddspharma.com and will be sent by post to shareholders who contact the Company and state their address.

About the meeting

The Board has appointed Daniel Lifveredson to open the meeting.

Proposed agenda

  • Opening of the meeting.
  • Election of chairman at the meeting.
  • Preparation and approval of the voting list.
  • Approval of the agenda.
  • Election of one or two persons to verify the minutes.
  • Examination of whether the meeting has been duly convened.
  • Resolution on amendment of the articles of association.
  • Resolution to reduce the share capital.
  • Closing of the meeting.

Proposed resolutions

Item 2: The Board of Directors proposes to elect Daniel Lifveredson as chairman of the meeting.

Item 7: The Board of Directors proposes that the general meeting resolve to adopt an updated version of the articles of association, whereby the limits for the Company's share capital are reduced. The reason for the Board's proposal is to enable the reduction in share capital proposed under item 8 on the agenda for the meeting.

The proposed amendment means that the Company's share capital shall amount to a minimum of SEK 500,000 and a maximum of SEK 2,000,000. After the amendment, the articles of association will have the wording set out in appendix 7.

The resolution in accordance with this proposal is conditional upon the meeting resolving to reduce the share capital in accordance with item 8 on the agenda.

The Board further proposes that the Board, or the person appointed by the Board, be authorised to make the minor formal adjustments to the articles of association that may prove necessary in connection with registration with the Swedish Companies Registration Office.

Item 8: The Board of Directors proposes that the general meeting resolve to reduce the Company’s share capital to cover losses as follows:

  • The share capital shall be reduced to cover the accumulated loss.
  • The share capital shall be reduced by SEK 6,732,556.278. After the reduction, the share capital shall amount to SEK 500,000.
  • The reduction of the share capital shall be carried out without the retirement of shares, which means that the quota value of the shares will be reduced.
  • The resolution in accordance with this proposal is conditional upon the general meeting resolving to amend the articles of association in accordance with item 7 on the agenda.

The Board further proposes that the Board, or the person appointed by the Board, be authorised to make any minor formal adjustments to the resolution that may be necessary in connection with registration with the Swedish Companies Registration Office.

Other information

Number of shares and votes

At the time of issuing this notice, the total number of shares in the Company amounts to 136,463,326. The Company does not hold any of its own shares, which means that all shares carry voting rights.

Majority requirements

A valid resolution under item 7 and 8 requires that the proposals are supported by shareholders with at least two thirds of both the votes cast and the shares represented at the general meeting.

Meeting documents

The Board of Directors' complete proposals, together with related documents, will be available on the Company's website, www.liddspharma.com, no later than two weeks before the meeting.

All of the documents above will be sent free of charge to the shareholders who request them from the Company and state their address and will be available on the website.

Right of question

Shareholders are informed of their right pursuant to Chapter 7, Section 32 of the Swedish Companies Act (2005: 551) to request information from the Board of Directors and the CEO at the general meeting regarding circumstances that may affect the assessment of an item on the agenda and circumstances that may affect the Company's and the group's financial situation.

Processing of personal data

For information on how your personal data is processed, please see: https://www.euroclear.com/dam/ESw/Legal/Integritetspolicy-bolagsstammor-svenska.pdf.

___________________________

Gothenburg in November 2025

The Board of Directors of LIDDS AB (publ)

data.title: Trump has not yet filed lawsuit against BBC, Trump's legal team says
data.description:
Donald Trump's legal team said on Thursday the U.S. president has not yet filed a lawsuit against the BBC over the broadcaster's editing of a speech he made in 2021 on the day his supporters overran the Capitol.

On Sunday, Trump sent a letter to the BBC threatening legal action against the broadcaster, which this week said the editing amounted to an "error of judgment." The letter demanded the BBC issue an apology and compensate Trump for damages.

On Wednesday, White House spokeswoman Karoline Leavitt said Trump's external legal counsel had filed the lawsuit. The White House did not immediately respond to a subsequent request for comment on that statement.

A spokesperson for Trump's legal team said the letter included "a requested response deadline (from the BBC) of Friday, Nov. 14," but added that Trump's lawyers reserve the right to file their lawsuit before then if they choose.

data.title: Bitcoin, Ethereum now operate in ‘different monetary’ universes: Data
data.description:
Bitcoin (BTC) and Ether (ETH) continue to diverge, and they currently operate in different monetary universes, according to a new joint report from Glassnode and Keyrock. The study noted that Bitcoin is drifting deeper into a savings-driven, low-velocity profile, while Ether is rapidly evolving into a productive onchain asset powering staking, collateral, and institutional wrappers.

Key takeaways:

  • Bitcoin’s dormancy and turnover now resemble gold far more than fiat.

  • Ether’s long-term holders are spending coins 3 times faster than BTC holders.

  • Both assets are leaving exchanges for ETFs, DATs, and staking at accelerating rates.

Bitcoin locks up, Ether speeds up

Glassnode highlighted that 61% of Bitcoin hasn’t moved in a year, with turnover at just 0.61% of free float per day, one of the lowest-velocity profiles among major global assets. “Bitcoin sits firmly in Store-of-Value territory,” the report noted, behaving more like gold than money in motion.

However, Ether is shifting in the opposite direction. ETH long-term holders are mobilizing dormant coins three times faster than BTC holders, a pattern Keyrock explained reflects “utility-driven behavior rather than hoarding.”

ETH’s turnover sits around 1.3% per day, double Bitcoin’s, and 1 in 4 Ether is now locked in staking or ETFs, creating a massive productive float that continues to power DeFi and liquid staking systems.

Exchange balances for both assets are collapsing—BTC by 1.5%, ETH by almost 18%, as coins flow into spot ETFs and digital asset investment vehicles. Analysts say this migration into “sticky” institutional custody may be the most important structural shift as Bitcoin is becoming more like a digital savings bond, while Ether is becoming the operational backbone of onchain activity.

Related: 3 reasons Bitcoin struggles to overcome each new overhead resistance level

Analysts see rising structural risk in ETH against BTC

Despite this widening behavioral gap, some analysts interpret the BTC–ETH dynamic in very different ways. Rather than viewing Ethereum’s high activity as a sign of strength, 10x Research argued it may reflect structural fragility, especially as Bitcoin continues to dominate institutional treasury flows.

A recent 10x report suggested that shorting ETH could serve as a hedge against Bitcoin’s rising institutional momentum. The firm claimed Ether-focused companies are running low on dry powder, weakening the “digital asset treasury” narrative that once drove accumulation.

Citing BitMine as an example, the researchers noted that certain treasury structures enabled institutions to acquire ETH at a low cost and later sell it to retail investors at a premium, a cycle they believe is now breaking down.

Although strong ETH inflows into Ether Treasuries held by companies have stagnated in Q4 (for context, it increased 124% in Q3), Bitmine continued to add more ETH to its allocation, increasing its total to 3,505,723 ETH, adding 110,288 ETH on Nov. 10.

Related: Altcoin index metric hints at early revival: Is the next rally close?

This article does not contain investment advice or recommendations. Every investment and trading move involves risk, and readers should conduct their own research when making a decision.

data.title: Latin American markets fall as focus shifts to upcoming U.S. data
data.description:
Key points:
  • LatAm stocks down 0.9%, FX flat
  • Brazil's retail sales miss forecast in September
  • Chile's central bank saw holding rate as the only option, minutes show
  • Peru's interest rate decision later in the day

By Nikhil Sharma and Sukriti Gupta

Latin American assets reversed early gains and fell on Thursday, tracking downbeat global markets as investors shifted focus to upcoming U.S. economic data after the nation's longest government shutdown ended.

MSCI's index for Latin American equities (.MILA00000PUS) fell 0.9% after a 0.8% drop in the previous session.

An equivalent gauge for currencies (.MILA00000CUS) was little changed after hitting an all-time high earlier in the day.

The dollar index slipped 0.4% on expectations of higher volatility from a backlog of data that had clouded the Federal Reserve's interest-rate outlook.

"You have had a bit more of a consistent risk-off session. It started with some resilience in general. Also, a sell-off in treasuries with some steepening, and then that has exacerbated throughout the U.S. session...so at some point a bit of the contagion has reached Latam," said Alejandro Cuadrado, global head of FX and LatAm strategy at BBVA.

Major U.S. stock indexes fell as investors scaled back expectations for interest-rate cuts on inflation worries and divisions among central bankers over the U.S. economy's health.

Late on Wednesday, U.S. President Donald Trump signed legislation ending the shutdown, allowing federal agencies to resume collecting data crucial to policymaking.

Peruvian markets braced for a policy decision at 1800 ET, with broad expectations the central bank will keep the rate at 4.25%.

In Brazil, the real eased 0.1% and the main stock index lost 0.6%.

The real has gained 14.3% this year, helped by near two-decade highs in interest rates, though high borrowing costs have weighed on growth, as shown by data pointing to an unexpected September retail sales decline from August.

Most Latin American assets have outperformed in 2025, outpacing Wall Street as investors increase exposure to emerging markets for better returns.

A study said major emerging economies, including Brazil and Mexico, can weather U.S. tariffs with limited damage, citing Mexico's policy and infrastructure resilience and Brazil's expanding trade ties.

Mexico's local equity index fell 0.3%, while the currency slipped 0.2%.

Chile's peso inched up 0.1% and the main equity index fell 1.9%. The country's central bank said the unanimous decision to keep the benchmark interest rate on hold at 4.75% was the "only plausible option" at its October meeting, the minutes showed.

In Argentina, the local peso =RASL> edged up 0.1% and the benchmark stock index fell 4.2%.

Elsewhere, the South African rand rose 0.3% to near a three-year high after the previous day's budget review, which the government said lowered the inflation target to 3%, the first such change in 25 years.

The Johannesburg Stock Exchange's Top-40 index rose 1.2% to a record high on speculation S&P Global Ratings may upgrade South Africa's sovereign rating at its review on Friday.

data.title: U.S. Kicks Off Winter With Ample Natural Gas Stocks, But Tighter Market Seen — Analysis
data.description:
By Anthony Harrup

The first arrival of frosty weather across the eastern U.S. this week sent natural gas futures to multimonth highs in a preview of what can happen to heating costs if a winter is particularly harsh.

The U.S. stores up natural gas in the spring and summer to cover winter demand, with natural gas the principal heating fuel for 46% of homes.

At the end of October, when underground caverns are usually at their fullest, inventories stood at 3,915 billion cubic feet, which was 162 Bcf or 4.3% above the average from 2020-2024.

Even with robust supplies to start the winter, with further expansion of liquefied natural gas export terminals and the possibility of production freeze-offs, stocks are likely to fall below average by midwinter, said Eli Rubin, senior energy analyst at EBW Analytics.

"Winter weather is really the 800-pound gorilla that swings it one way or another as a known unknown factor," he said.

The U.S. exited last winter with natural gas inventories about 10% below the five-year average, leading to concerns about this winter's supply given prospects of increased demand from LNG exporters and power plants. But after a mild spring and early start to the storage refill season, the deficit had turned to a surplus by the start of the summer.

Extreme heat in July and the first part of August raised electricity demand for air conditioning, but the call on natural gas was offset by higher production of renewable energy, especially wind, said Dennis Kissler, senior vice president of trading at BOK Financial.

There was also a lot of maintenance carried out on LNG terminals, where gas is cooled into liquid form for shipment abroad. "So we saw an enormous amount of LNG back up that normally should have left. And production of course was a record level," Kissler said.

The U.S. Energy Information Administration expects natural gas spot prices to average $3.90 per million British thermal units this winter at Henry Hub in Louisiana, with a peak of $4.25/mmBtu in January. Henry Hub connects a network of pipelines. It serves as the delivery point for natural gas futures contracts traded on the New York Mercantile Exchange, and sets a benchmark for regional pricing. The EIA expects residential prices to rise 2% from last winter to about $13.80 per 1,000 cubic feet.

Nymex natural gas traded around $4.68/mmBtu Thursday, after trading below $3 for much of August and September, supported by this week's low temperatures across the eastern U.S. and expectations of a cold December.

"Fundamentals suggest a slightly tighter market through the winter," Andy Huenefeld, managing partner at Pinebrook Energy Advisors, said in a report. But "current prices appear to embed an outsized risk premium for cold weather and potential supply disruptions."

The U.S. natural gas market shows significant flexibility. After an unusually warm 2023-2024 winter, stocks were 40% above average. But low prices that year led producers to curtail output and power generators to favor natural gas, and by the end of the 2024 injection season the surplus was down to around 5%.

"It's prices that make that happen," said Rubin of EBW Analytics. "Storage gets to the same level at the end of the injection period every year, plus or minus 200 Bcf. What gets us there is prices move to create the right incentives, so storage ends up in the right place."

Growing LNG Presents Distribution Challenges

While U.S. producers have demonstrated the ability to lift production, getting the gas to where it's needed presents a challenge. The EIA expects LNG exports to rise 10% in 2026 to a record 16.3 billion cubic feet a day.

"We have many different areas that produce natural gas but they're not all connected. So we almost have stranded gas plays around the country, and I think we're going to see larger disparities in the price of natural gas across the different regions of the U.S.," said Shon Hiatt, director of the Zage Business of Energy Initiative at the USC Marshall School of Business.

New pipelines need to be built to supply LNG terminals, with the U.S. looking to double its capacity by 2029, according to the EIA.

"With the gas distribution network already constrained, the U.S. gas market could face supply bottlenecks, price volatility, and a growing dependence on higher-cost supply basins," analysts at McKinsey said in a report. "The Appalachian Basin contains an abundant, low-cost dry gas supply but faces severe pipeline bottlenecks that limit the flow of gas to high-demand markets."

Write to Anthony Harrup at anthony.harrup@wsj.com

data.title: Terreno Realty Is Maintained at Neutral by Citigroup
Skyline Bankshares, Inc. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Skyline Bankshares, Inc., a prominent player in the retail and commercial banking sector, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter of 2025. The report highlights significant financial growth and strategic business developments, underscoring the company's robust performance and future outlook.

Financial Highlights

  • Interest Income: $16.874 million for the three months ended September 30, 2025, reflecting an increase from $13.712 million in the same period in 2024, driven by higher loan balances and increased interest rates.
  • Net Interest Income: $12.891 million for the three months ended September 30, 2025, up from $9.931 million in the same period in 2024, indicating improved interest margins.
  • Net Income: $4.076 million for the three months ended September 30, 2025, compared to $1.057 million in the same period in 2024, showcasing significant growth in profitability.
  • Net Income Per Share: $0.73 for the three months ended September 30, 2025, up from $0.19 in the same period in 2024, reflecting enhanced earnings per share.
  • Noninterest Income: $1.961 million for the three months ended September 30, 2025, compared to $1.851 million in the same period in 2024, driven by increased service charges and fees.
  • Noninterest Expenses: $9.565 million for the three months ended September 30, 2025, slightly down from $9.625 million in the same period in 2024, indicating effective cost management.
  • Provision for Credit Losses: $189 thousand for the three months ended September 30, 2025, down from $738 thousand in the same period in 2024, reflecting improved credit quality.

Business Highlights

  • Segment Reporting: The company operates as a single segment, focusing on retail and commercial banking services through its wholly-owned subsidiary, Skyline National Bank. This structure allows for a unified approach to serving a diverse client base with similar products and services.
  • Geographical Performance: Skyline National Bank serves multiple counties across Virginia, North Carolina, and Tennessee, with a network of twenty-eight full-service banking offices and two loan production offices. This regional presence supports the bank's strategy to cater to local market needs and expand its footprint in Eastern Tennessee following the acquisition of Johnson County Bank.
  • Business Combination: The acquisition of Johnson County Bank in September 2024 was a strategic move to enter the Eastern Tennessee market. This acquisition added $154.1 million in assets and $133.8 million in liabilities to the company's balance sheet, enhancing its market presence and customer base.
  • Loan Portfolio: The company's loan portfolio is diversified across real estate secured loans, including construction, development, farmland, residential, and commercial mortgages, as well as non-real estate secured loans such as commercial, agricultural, and consumer loans. This diversification helps mitigate risk and supports stable growth.
  • Allowance for Credit Losses: The allowance for credit losses increased due to higher loan volumes and changes in forecast variables, reflecting the company's proactive approach to managing credit risk in a dynamic economic environment.
  • Future Outlook: Management anticipates continued growth in its core markets, leveraging its expanded geographical presence and diversified loan portfolio. The company remains focused on maintaining strong credit quality and capitalizing on opportunities for organic growth and strategic acquisitions.

SEC Filing: Skyline Bankshares, Inc. [ SLBK ] - 10-Q - Nov. 13, 2025

data.title: Oak Valley Bancorp SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Oak Valley Bancorp, a community-oriented bank operating primarily in California's Central Valley and Eastern Sierras, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter of 2025. The report highlights the company's financial performance and operational activities, providing insights into its revenue streams, geographical performance, and future outlook.

Financial Highlights

Total Interest Income: Oak Valley Bancorp reported a total interest income of $22.517 million for the three months ended September 30, 2025, an increase from $21.146 million in the same period of 2024. This growth was driven by higher interest and fees on loans.

Net Interest Income: The net interest income for the three months ended September 30, 2025, was $19.197 million, up from $17.655 million in the same period of 2024. This increase was attributed to the growth in earning assets and the upward repricing of loan yields.

Net Income: The company reported a net income of $6.693 million for the three months ended September 30, 2025, compared to $7.324 million in the same period of 2024. The decrease was primarily due to higher operating expenses.

Net Income Per Share: Net income per share was $0.81 for the three months ended September 30, 2025, down from $0.89 in the same period of 2024, reflecting the decrease in net income.

Net Income: For the nine months ended September 30, 2025, net income was $17.578 million, compared to $18.940 million in the same period of 2024, influenced by increased operating expenses.

Net Income Per Share: Net income per share for the nine months ended September 30, 2025, was $2.13, compared to $2.30 in the same period of 2024.

Net Interest Income: The net interest income for the nine months ended September 30, 2025, was $55.158 million, an increase from $52.188 million in the same period of 2024, due to higher earning asset balances.

Non-Interest Income: Non-interest income for the three months ended September 30, 2025, was $1.973 million, up from $1.846 million in the same period of 2024, mainly due to a one-time gain from a life insurance policy redemption.

Non-Interest Expense: Non-interest expense for the three months ended September 30, 2025, was $12.700 million, up from $11.324 million in the same period of 2024, primarily due to staffing increases and overhead related to servicing growing business portfolios.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments: The primary source of revenue for Oak Valley Bancorp is providing loans to middle-market businesses. Approximately 87% of loans are commercial real estate loans, including construction loans. About 7% of loans are for general commercial use, 3% for residential real estate and other consumer loans, and 3% for agriculture loans.

Geographical Performance: The company operates in the California Central Valley and Eastern Sierras, with branches in Oakdale, Sonora, Bridgeport, Bishop, Mammoth Lakes, Modesto, Manteca, Patterson, Turlock, Ripon, Stockton, Escalon, Roseville, Lodi, and Sacramento. The Eastern Sierra Community Bank operates as a division within Oak Valley Community Bank for select branches.

Sales Units: Oak Valley Bancorp reported a net increase in loans, with total loans reaching $1,112,829,000 as of September 30, 2025, up from $1,106,535,000 at the end of 2024.

Future Outlook: Management anticipates continued growth in earning asset balances and plans to maintain competitive interest rates on deposit products to sustain liquidity levels. The company is focused on operating as a well-capitalized, profitable, and independent community-oriented bank, with a strategy to enhance shareholder value and efficiently utilize capital.

SEC Filing: Oak Valley Bancorp [ OVLY ] - 10-Q - Nov. 13, 2025

data.title: LogicMark, Inc. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
LogicMark, Inc., a company specializing in personal emergency response systems (PERS) and other personal safety devices, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter of 2025. The report highlights the company's financial performance and operational developments, reflecting its ongoing efforts to modernize remote monitoring and enhance personal safety.

Financial Highlights

Revenue: LogicMark, Inc. reported revenue of $2.9 million for the three months ended September 30, 2025, marking an 8% increase compared to the same period in 2024. For the nine months ended September 30, 2025, revenue reached $8.4 million, a 9% increase from the previous year.

Gross Profit: The company achieved a gross profit of $1.9 million for the three months ended September 30, 2025, maintaining a profit margin of 66%, consistent with the prior year. For the nine months ended September 30, 2025, gross profit was $5.5 million, also with a 66% profit margin.

Operating Loss: LogicMark, Inc. reported an operating loss of $(1.7) million for the three months ended September 30, 2025, compared to $(1.6) million in the same period in 2024. The operating loss for the nine months ended September 30, 2025, was $(6.2) million, compared to $(5.5) million in the same period in 2024.

Net Loss: The net loss for the three months ended September 30, 2025, was $(1.6) million, compared to $(1.5) million in the same period in 2024. For the nine months ended September 30, 2025, the net loss was $(5.9) million, compared to $(5.3) million in the same period in 2024.

Net Loss Attributable to Common Stockholders Per Share - Basic and Diluted: The net loss per share for the three months ended September 30, 2025, was $(2.21), a significant improvement from $(3,732.56) in the same period in 2024. For the nine months ended September 30, 2025, the net loss per share was $(11.98), compared to $(25,132.66) in the same period in 2024.

Business Highlights

Product Line Performance: The increase in revenue was driven by higher sales of the Freedom Alert Mini units, launched in 2024, and the recently upgraded Guardian Alert 911 Plus.

Operational Focus: LogicMark, Inc. continues to focus on modernizing remote monitoring to help people stay safe and live independently longer. The company's PERS technologies and other personal safety devices are sold through various channels, including direct-to-consumer, eCommerce, and the United States Veterans Health Administration.

Sales Channels: The company is shifting its sales strategy from business-to-consumer to business-to-business channels, impacting advertising costs and sales strategies.

Personnel Changes: The increase in selling and marketing expenses was driven by the hiring of a new Senior Vice President of Sales and Vice President of Business Development, indicating a strategic focus on expanding sales capabilities.

Research and Development: There was an increase in research and development costs, primarily driven by an increase in personnel wages, reflecting the company's commitment to innovation and product development.

Future Outlook: LogicMark, Inc. believes it will have sufficient capital to sustain operations for at least twelve months from the date of the filing. The company has potential plans to raise additional funds through equity or debt offerings to accelerate its long-term strategic plan.

SEC Filing: LogicMark, Inc. [ LGMK ] - 10-Q - Nov. 13, 2025

data.title: Franklin Crypto Trust SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Franklin Crypto Trust, a fund focused on digital assets, has released its Q3 2025 10-Q report. The report provides insights into the fund's financial and operational performance, highlighting its investment strategy, digital asset holdings, and future outlook. The Franklin Crypto Trust aims to track the CF Institutional Digital Asset Index, primarily focusing on bitcoin and ether.

Financial Highlights

Unfortunately, the provided text does not contain specific financial metrics such as Revenue, Gross Profit, Operating Income, Net Income, and Net Income Per Share. The report appears to focus more on the operational aspects and the fund's digital asset holdings rather than traditional financial performance metrics.

Business Highlights

Fund Launch and Operations Commencement

The Franklin Crypto Trust was formed on August 13, 2024, and the Franklin Crypto Index ETF commenced operations on February 20, 2025. The Fund aims to track the CF Institutional Digital Asset Index, focusing on bitcoin and ether.

Investment Strategy

The Fund employs a passive investment strategy, seeking to reflect the price of its Underlying Index by investing in digital assets in the same weights as they appear in the Index. This approach is designed to closely correspond to the performance of the Index before expenses and liabilities.

Digital Asset Holdings

As of September 30, 2025, the Fund's digital asset holdings consisted of 55.4156 bitcoins and 304.3726 ethers, with a total fair value of $7,587,051. The assets are weighted based on their free float market capitalization.

Custodial and Administrative Services

The Bank of New York Mellon serves as the Fund’s Administrator, Transfer Agent, and Cash Custodian, while Coinbase Custody Trust Company, LLC is responsible for the safekeeping of digital assets.

Creation and Redemption of Shares

The Fund issues and redeems shares in blocks of 50,000, known as Creation Units, which are directly redeemable only by Authorized Participants. These transactions are conducted in cash.

Sponsor's Fee and Waiver

The Sponsor's fee is set at an annualized rate of 0.19% of the Fund's net asset value. For the period from February 20, 2025, to August 29, 2025, the Sponsor waived the entire fee on the first $10 billion of the Fund’s assets.

Market Listing and Trading

The Fund's shares are listed and traded on the Cboe BZX Exchange under the ticker symbol 'EZPZ'. The market price of the shares may differ from the Fund’s NAV per share.

Risk Concentration

The Fund's exclusive investment in digital assets exposes it to concentration risk, with potential adverse effects from fluctuations in digital asset prices, market conditions, and regulatory changes.

Future Outlook

The Fund aims to continue its passive investment strategy, maintaining its focus on tracking the performance of the CF Institutional Digital Asset Index. The management anticipates ongoing volatility in digital asset prices, which could impact the Fund's performance.

SEC Filing: Franklin Crypto Trust [ EZPZ ] - 10-Q - Nov. 13, 2025

data.title: CoinShares Bitcoin ETF SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
CoinShares Bitcoin ETF, a Delaware statutory trust listed on Nasdaq under the ticker symbol BRRR, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter of 2025. The report highlights significant financial gains and robust operational performance, driven primarily by the appreciation in the value of bitcoin and strategic share activities.

Financial Highlights

Net Income: The company reported a net income of $43.33 million for the three months ended September 30, 2025, a substantial increase from $12.63 million for the same period in 2024. This growth was primarily driven by realized and unrealized gains on bitcoin investments.

Net Income Per Share: Net income per share rose to $2.08 for the three months ended September 30, 2025, compared to $0.41 for the same period in 2024, indicating improved profitability per share.

Net Income (Nine Months): For the nine months ended September 30, 2025, net income was $107.86 million, up from $33.56 million for the same period in 2024. This increase was primarily due to substantial realized gains on bitcoin sales.

Net Income Per Share (Nine Months): Net income per share for the nine months ended September 30, 2025, was $4.64, up from $1.37 for the same period in 2024, highlighting enhanced earnings per share.

Business Highlights

Trust Overview: CoinShares Bitcoin ETF, formerly known as CoinShares Valkyrie Bitcoin Fund, is a Delaware statutory trust that issues shares representing units of fractional undivided beneficial interest in its net assets. The Trust's shares are listed on Nasdaq under the ticker symbol BRRR.

Investment Objective: The Trust aims to reflect the performance of the value of bitcoin as represented by the CME CF Bitcoin Reference Rate – New York Variant, less the Trust’s liabilities and expenses.

Operational Strategy: The Trust holds bitcoin and values its shares daily based on the value of bitcoin as reflected by the Index, which aggregates executed trade flow from major bitcoin spot exchanges.

Share Activity: During the three months ended September 30, 2025, the Trust saw an increase in outstanding shares from 20,705,000 to 21,120,000, with 1,160,000 shares created and 745,000 shares redeemed.

Bitcoin Valuation: The Trust uses the CME CF Bitcoin Reference Rate – New York Variant to evaluate its bitcoin holdings and determine the net asset value of the Trust and its shares.

Market Performance: The net asset value per share increased by 6.9% from $30.22 at June 30, 2025, to $32.31 at September 30, 2025, driven by a 7.0% appreciation in the value of bitcoin.

Future Outlook: The Trust continues to focus on reflecting the performance of bitcoin and maintaining its operational strategy of holding bitcoin and valuing shares based on the Index. The Sponsor is authorized to substitute an alternative index or methodology for valuing bitcoin if necessary.

SEC Filing: CoinShares Bitcoin ETF [ BRRR ] - 10-Q - Nov. 13, 2025

data.title: CS DIAGNOSTICS CORP. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
CS DIAGNOSTICS CORP., a leading company in the diagnostic services industry, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter of 2025. The report highlights the company's financial performance and operational activities, providing insights into its revenue streams, cost management, and future outlook.

Financial Highlights

Total Revenue: $94,200,000, representing a decrease of approximately 11% compared to the same period in 2024. This decline is primarily attributed to reduced diagnostic service activity and lower one-time licensing income.

Net Income: $32,081,000, an improvement from $13,448,000 in the prior-year period. The increase in net income is mainly due to a reduction in operating expenses and the absence of significant financing or debt-related costs.

Basic and Diluted Loss Per Share: $0.00, reflecting the company's efforts in maintaining operational efficiency and cost control.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments: The company experienced a decrease in total revenues for the nine months ended September 30, 2025, primarily due to reduced diagnostic service activity and lower one-time licensing income compared to the previous year.

Geographical Performance: CS DIAGNOSTICS CORP. is focusing on increasing market penetration in the United States, Europe, and MENA regions. This strategic focus is expected to stabilize and potentially increase revenues in the future.

Operational Efficiency: The company has significantly reduced its operating expenses by completing several one-time legal, audit, and consulting engagements in 2024. Additionally, resources have been shifted from product development to commercialization, further enhancing operational efficiency.

Future Outlook: Management anticipates moderate revenue growth and improved cash flow in the second half of 2025. This positive outlook is driven by newly executed licensing agreements beginning to yield results and continued strengthening of operational efficiencies.

SEC Filing: CS DIAGNOSTICS CORP. [ CSDX ] - 10-Q - Nov. 13, 2025

data.title: PLATTS: 350--Platts Power US Southeast Daily Commentary - 13Nov25
data.description: (Platts)--13Nov2025/313 pm EST/2013 GMT The Electric Reliability Council of Texas saw next-day trading activity on the Intercontinental Exchange Nov. 13 tick up, tracking demand fundamentals and above-average temperatures. The ERCOT North Hub's day-ahead on-peak package rose around $1.75 to price at $30/MWh for Nov. 14 delivery. The hub's real-time package traded $1 higher at $31/MWh, falling around $2 from its previous ICE weighted average. In the futures market, off-peak December packages were trading around $2 higher on Nov. 13, priced at $46.75/MWh. In the on-peak market, power trades for December rose around $3.75 to $54/MWh. MARKET FUNDAMENTALS Moving in line with rising prices, ERCOT expects peakload demand to rise 4.1% to 59.48 GW on Nov. 14. Increases come as solar generation was forecast to decrease, falling 12.32 GWh, or 6.7%, to 172.32 GWh, according to ERCOT's solar forecast data. Furthermore, temperatures in Dallas and Houston were forecast to rise three degrees and one degree, respectively, expected around 80 F (27 C) and 82 F Nov. 14, according to CustomWeather. These temperature highs were observed 12 F above Dallas' five-day norm and 10 F above Houston's, also according to CustomWeather, with above-average temperatures aiding bullish pricing trends. NATURAL GAS In natural gas pricing Nov. 13, Transco Zone 5 South saw ICE's next-day delivery trades fall 16 cents to $3.44/MMBtu, aiding in reducing the bullish market fundamentals typically observed through rising peakload demand and temperatures. — Ricardo Plata iii, ricardo.plata.iii@spglobal.com-- Edited by Ashanti Rojano, newsdesk@spglobal.com--Platts Electricity Alert--

data.title: Grand Rapids to Issue $175M Bond to Fund Senior Living Expansion
data.description:
By Paulo Trevisani

The Economic Development Corporation of the City of Grand Rapids, Mich., plans to issue $174.83 million in bonds to finance the expansion of the Beacon Hill at Eastgate senior living community.

The proceeds will fund the demolition, acquisition, construction, furnishing, and equipping of new senior housing and healthcare facilities, while also covering other obligations, according to a preliminary official statement published Thursday on MuniOs.

The expansion will include 101 new independent living units and 40 assisted living units, while also renovating existing healthcare facilities.

The Limited Obligation Revenue Bonds are tax-exempt and will have no coupons.

The issuance is divided into four tranches. The Series 2025A Fixed Rate Bonds total $107.83 million and mature through 2060. They carry assumed interest rates ranging from 5.825% to 6.1% a year.

Series 2025B-1 TEMPS-85SM amounts to $12.25 million, Series 2025B-2 TEMPS-70SM to $15.75 million and Series 2025B-3 TEMPS-50SM totals $39 million. They all mature in 2030 and carry an assumed interest rate of 4.625% a year.

The bonds are payable from the revenues and funds pledged by the Michigan Christian Home Obligated Group.

The Obligor Group's total revenue, gains and other support for the fiscal year ended March 31 was $21.1 million.

The bonds are not rated. The underwriter is Ziegler.

Write to Paulo Trevisani at paulo.trevisani@wsj.com

data.title: Deputy Director of Enforcement Antonia M. Apps to Conclude Her Tenure at the SEC
data.description:
Washington, D.C.--(Newsfile Corp. - November 13, 2025) - The Securities and Exchange Commission today announced that Antonia M. Apps, Deputy Director of the Division of Enforcement (Northeast), will conclude her tenure with the agency effective Dec. 1, 2025.

“I thank Antonia for her steadfast leadership in her different roles in the Division of Enforcement and her commitment to the staff and the SEC’s mission,” said SEC Division of Enforcement Director Margaret A. Ryan. “Her strategic counsel and analysis, her willingness to take on complex issues, and her dedication to the staff and the agency have earned her the deep respect of her colleagues during her tenure at the SEC. I wish Antonia all the best in her next endeavor.”

Ms. Apps said, “I want to thank Acting Chairman Mark Uyeda for entrusting me with the opportunity to help lead the exceptionally talented staff of the Division of Enforcement, first as Acting Deputy Director for the entire Division and then as Deputy Director overseeing the New York, Boston, Philadelphia, and Chicago regional offices. I am proud of the Division’s collaboration with colleagues across the agency and with outside government partners to advance the Commission’s mission of protecting investors and promoting fairness and integrity in our markets. It has been a privilege to help lead the Division this year and to serve as head of the New York Regional Office for the preceding two years. Throughout my tenure, I have been consistently inspired by the dedication, integrity, and resilience of the staff who define this great agency. As I move on to my next chapter, I remain confident that this Division and the SEC will continue to meet the evolving challenges of our markets with determination, skill, and commitment to the public interest.”

Ms. Apps served as Regional Director of the New York Regional Office from January 2023 through January 2025. In this role, she oversaw an office of over 600 attorneys, accountants, investigators, securities compliance examiners and others, and led the examination and enforcement programs for the New York region. Under Ms. Apps’ leadership, the New York Office brought more stand-alone enforcement actions and completed more examinations than any other office in the country, and hired more staff in a two-year period than were hired in any previous two-year period in the history of the New York office. With her support, the New York office also engaged in more investor education and outreach events than ever before.

In January 2025, Ms. Apps was appointed Acting Deputy Director of the Division of Enforcement and, subsequently, Deputy Director of Enforcement (Northeast), helping to lead the Division of Enforcement on a national level.

Before joining the SEC as Regional Director in 2023, Ms. Apps was a litigation partner at Milbank LLP, where she represented clients in high-stakes criminal, regulatory, and complex civil matters. She previously served as an Assistant U.S. Attorney in the Criminal Division of the Southern District of New York, where she investigated and prosecuted high-profile securities fraud and other white-collar cases. Since 2016, Ms. Apps has also taught white-collar criminal law and procedure at Harvard Law School. Earlier in her career, Ms. Apps was a partner at Kellogg, Hansen, Todd, Figel & Frederick PLLC in Washington, D.C., where she represented clients in complex litigation and regulatory matters, and an associate at Fried, Frank, Harris, Shriver & Jacobson LLP in New York. She also served as a law clerk to the Honorable Fred I. Parker of the U.S. Court of Appeals for the Second Circuit, and holds law degrees from the universities of Sydney, Oxford, and Harvard.

data.title: Key facts: Bloom Energy's stock surges; Tradr launches leveraged ETFs
data.description:
• Bloom Energy's stock has risen fivefold this year, driven by demand for on-site power, particularly from data centers. Partnerships with firms like Oracle enhance its fuel cell deployment.1
  • Tradr has launched leveraged ETFs that include Bloom Energy Corp, alongside companies like Celestica, NANO Nuclear, and Synopsys.2

data.title: Key facts: BlackRock nears $26.8B ACS deal; exceeds voting rights limits
data.description:
• BlackRock's Global Infrastructure Partners is nearing a $26.8 billion deal with Spain's ACS to develop data centers, acquiring a 50% stake in ACS's Digital & Energy unit.12
  • On November 12, 2025, BlackRock, Inc. exceeded a regulatory limit for voting rights in IP Group PLC. It also crossed a similar threshold for Helios Towers PLC on November 11, 2025.3
  • BlackRock has partnered with RIQ to develop reinsurance solutions for clean energy projects, enhancing coverage and capital efficiency in the global energy transition.4

data.title: Key facts: Beyond Meat cuts 64 jobs; reports 13% retail volume drop; Mizuho lowers price target
data.description:
• Beyond Meat has cut its workforce by about 44 employees in 2025 and suspended operations in China, resulting in an extra reduction of 20 employees.1
  • Beyond Meat's third-quarter report showed a 13% decline in U.S. retail volumes and a 27% drop in foodservice volumes, accompanied by a $77 million impairment charge.2
  • Mizuho has reduced its price target for Beyond Meat (BYND) from $1.50 per share to $1.00 per share while maintaining its rating of 'Underperform'.3

data.title: Key facts: B of A Raises Trane Technologies Price Target to $550; Climate Week NYC 2025 Panel on Decarbonization
data.description:
• B of A Securities has increased the price target for Trane Technologies to $550.00 per share, up from the previous target of $490.00.1
  • Trane Technologies hosted a panel during Climate Week NYC 2025, focusing on decarbonization in the built environment, emphasizing collaboration and innovation for sustainability goals.2
